meta.runtime: Node.js single-process Discord bot runtime plus Vite-built React dashboard
meta.summary: The repository is a Node.js Discord moderation/automation bot with a static-config-centric architecture, PostgreSQL persistence, strong command/event test coverage, and an accompanying React dashboard that currently expects HTTP APIs not present in the scanned backend runtime entrypoint.

entrypoints.role: Primary backend bootstrap and Discord process lifecycle controller
entrypoints.notes: Validates config, runs migrations, acquires PG advisory singleton lock, logs into Discord, bootstraps schedulers/services, starts optional health listener, installs fatal/shutdown handlers.

entrypoints.role: Discord gateway client factory and event wiring hub
entrypoints.notes: Registers all runtime event listeners and routes them to moderation/reaction/private-room/tag-role/presence flows.
Bootstraps SPA with error boundary and routing.

style: Layered backend (bootstrap, bot, application, infrastructure, config)
Single-process stateful runtime with DB-backed durability

high_level_modules.name: Bootstrap and Process Lifecycle
high_level_modules.purpose: Startup sequencing, retries, singleton lock, shutdown and fatal handling

high_level_modules.name: Discord Event Router
high_level_modules.purpose: Central gateway event subscriptions and feature dispatch

high_level_modules.name: Moderation Command Runtime
high_level_modules.purpose: Prefix command parsing, command execution, permission gating, action side effects

high_level_modules.name: Timed Penalty Scheduler
high_level_modules.purpose: Persist and reconcile expiring moderation actions

high_level_modules.name: Reaction Action Engine
high_level_modules.purpose: Rule-driven automation on reaction add/remove with cooldowns and once-only semantics

high_level_modules.name: Private Voice Room Domain
high_level_modules.purpose: Auto-create/manage private voice channels and owner panel interactions

high_level_modules.name: Voice Connection Manager
high_level_modules.purpose: Deterministic connect/disconnect operations with verification and per-guild queueing

high_level_modules.name: Tag Role Feature
high_level_modules.purpose: Synchronize a configured role based on Discord profile primary guild identity state

high_level_modules.name: Configuration and Static Authority Layer
high_level_modules.purpose: Define and normalize immutable server settings/bindings and validate against live guild resources
used_by: all feature modules via cache/config helpers

high_level_modules.name: Dashboard Frontend
high_level_modules.purpose: Operational UI for settings/reaction rules/embed send/health checks

startup_flow: index.main installs console hooks and optional diagnostics timer registry/listener watchers.
runMigrations pings DB and executes ensurePostgresStartupSchema idempotent DDL.
createDiscordClient wires gateway listeners and returns Client instance.
construct feature services: presence manager, private room service, reaction service, tag-role feature.
optional health HTTP server starts when PORT is set.
acquire_bot_instance_lock uses PostgreSQL advisory lock to enforce singleton instance behavior.
validateStaticConfig checks static role/channel/emoji bindings against live guild.
bootstrap penalty scheduler and private room service; refresh reaction rules; run tag startup sync.
runtime_flow: messageCreate routes prefix commands first, then custom command cache fallback.
reaction add/remove events map to reaction action rules.
voiceStateUpdate drives private room create/reconcile flows.
interactionCreate routes embed builder IDs first, then private room panel actions.
member/user updates trigger tag role synchronization.
scheduled intervals reconcile penalties, private room cleanup, diagnostics/perf snapshots (feature-flagged).
shutdown_flow: penalty scheduler/private room/presence services shut down timers.
diagnostics watcher stop and timer registry report.
release PostgreSQL advisory lock and close DB pool.
process exits with code depending on signal/failure.
execution_model.notes: Startup uses retry/backoff for transient DB/static validation/bootstrap failures and is designed to wait until dependencies recover instead of immediate crash loops.

design_patterns_detected.notes: Prefix command names map to handler modules with unified ctx object injection.
Persistence logic separated from service/controller logic.
beforePrimaryAction, primaryAction, sideEffects, template/error routing, receipt commit/rollback.
Serializes conflicting updates by target/guild/channel/room keys to reduce race conditions.
Post-action state re-fetch with retry before reporting success.

sharding.details: No ShardingManager or shard process model detected; singleton enforced via PostgreSQL advisory lock.

gateway_events.purpose: Prefix moderation commands plus custom command responses
Tag role sync on member update
Tag role sync by primaryGuild identity changes
Embed builder and private room panel interactions
Startup cache load, presence apply, startup voice auto-join

registration_flow: Prefix commands are statically registered in commandHandlers map in api/src/bot/moderation.js.
Built-in command name list in api/src/bot/builtinCommands.js includes vcmute/vcunmute aliases, but no active handlers were found for these in the dispatcher.
dispatch_flow: moderation.handlePrefix parses prefix+command+args, resolves target, builds ctx, invokes handler.run(ctx).
Handlers call verifyPermission and executeModerationAction wrapper.
validation_flow: Command enabled/role/native permission checks in permissionService.verifyPermission.
Hierarchy/protected target checks via nativeHierarchy + hard protected config.
Execution preconditions: bot perms, target manageable/moderatable/bannable/kickable, managed role config.
error_flow: Command handlers use template sender for user-facing errors.
executeModerationAction maps Discord permission/hierarchy failures and records side-effect degradation.
Global process-level unhandledRejection/uncaughtException triggers controlled shutdown.

feature_inventory.description: Warn, mute/unmute, kick, jail/unjail, ban/unban, log, lock/unlock, status and help commands under a configurable prefix.
user_flow: Moderator sends prefixed command in guild text channel.
Bot validates permissions and command gates.
Bot executes moderation action and responds with templated embed/normal message.
Optional DM notification sent to target user on success.
internal_flow: messageCreate -> moderation.handlePrefix -> target resolution.
permissionService.verifyPermission performs command/native/hierarchy/rate checks.
executeModerationAction wraps primary action and side effects (logging/scheduler).
templateService renders configured/default response templates.

feature_inventory.feature_name: Timed Penalty Scheduler and Jail Role Snapshot Restore
feature_inventory.description: Schedules future unmute/unjail/vcunmute revocations and restores pre-jail role snapshots.
user_flow: Moderator applies timed jail/mute-like action.
Penalty is persisted with revoke_at timestamp.
Scheduler auto-revokes action when due or on startup reconcile.
internal_flow: penaltyScheduler.schedulePenalty upserts active penalty row.
In-memory timer map schedules due callback (bounded timeout for long delays).
executePenalty re-loads active row and revokes action, marking inactive.
Periodic reconcile interval rehydrates timers from DB.

feature_inventory.description: Ban/unban commands verify remote guild ban state with retries and mutation locks before success messaging.
user_flow: Moderator issues ban or unban command.
Bot checks current ban state, applies mutation, then verifies resulting state.
User-facing success returned only when final state is confirmed.
internal_flow: guildBanState.fetchAuthoritativeGuildBan uses force fetch and cache eviction for unknown ban errors.
ensureGuildBanPresent/Absent uses retryModerationVerification schedule.
ban/unban command uses acquireGuildBanMutationLock per guild+user.

feature_inventory.description: Configurable reaction triggers execute role/DM/reply/channel-link/internal-command/group cleanup actions with cooldown and only-once controls.
user_flow: Operator configures reaction rule (via expected API/UI path).
User adds/removes matching reaction.
Configured action pipeline executes if constraints pass.
internal_flow: reaction event -> safe fetch partials -> emoji normalization.
Rule cache lookup by guild/message/emoji key; miss cache throttles reloads.
Role constraints, cooldown checks, optional only-once reservation.
Action execution and per-event logging persistence.

feature_inventory.feature_name: Private Voice Room Lifecycle and Control Panel
feature_inventory.description: Auto-provisions owner-specific private rooms from a hub and provides interaction-driven lock/visibility/permit/reject/transfer/rename/limit/delete controls.
user_flow: Eligible user joins configured private hub voice channel.
Bot creates or reuses private voice room and posts panel controls.
Room owner uses buttons/selects/modals to manage room access and behavior.
internal_flow: voiceStateUpdate detects hub join/leave and room occupancy transitions.
Room data persisted in private_voice_rooms, cached in memory.
Interaction handlers enforce owner checks and current-panel checks.
Overwrite snapshots and runtime enforcement disconnect unauthorized members.

feature_inventory.description: Assigns/removes configured tag role based on user.primaryGuild identity fields.
user_flow: User profile identity changes or member updates occur.
Bot computes whether user should have tag role.
Bot adds/removes role if permissions and hierarchy allow.
internal_flow: guildMemberAdd/guildMemberUpdate/userUpdate events call syncTagRole.
Per-member lock avoids concurrent role mutation races.
Startup sync scans cached members for drift correction.

feature_inventory.description: Lock/unlock command captures text channel SendMessages overwrite snapshot and restores exactly on unlock.
user_flow: Moderator runs lock in text channel.
Bot denies @everyone send and neutralizes conflicting allows.
Moderator runs unlock; bot restores saved overwrite states.
internal_flow: channelLock.helpers enforces actor+bot manage-channel capability.
Snapshot persisted to text_channel_lock_snapshots table.
Verification checks lock/unlock convergence before reporting success.

feature_inventory.description: Starts a button+modal flow to send custom embeds to a selected text channel.
user_flow: Moderator runs embed command optionally with target channel and text.
Bot responds with button to open modal.
Moderator submits modal fields and bot posts embed to target channel.
internal_flow: In-memory tokenized flow map with TTL.
Interaction actor validation and permission re-check.
Input validation for color/url/content lengths before send.

feature_inventory.description: Allows cached custom command text responses loaded from DB.
user_flow: User sends matching prefixed or plain custom command trigger.
Bot replies with stored text response.
internal_flow: cache.loadAllCustomCommands loads custom_commands into memory on ready.
discordClient checks custom command only when builtin prefix handler did not handle message.

feature_inventory.description: Attempts to connect bot to configured startup voice channels after ready.
user_flow: Bot reaches ready state.
Auto-joiner attempts verified voice join to configured channels.
internal_flow: Collect target guild/channel IDs from static config and env fallbacks.
Validate guild/channel/permissions before connect.
Use voiceManager verified connect with retry policy.

feature_inventory.description: React UI for moderation settings, reaction rules, embeds, guild resources, and health status.
user_flow: User logs in via /api/auth/login.
Dashboard loads session and guild context.
UI issues API requests for settings/rules/health/actions.
internal_flow: useDashboardData orchestrates multi-endpoint data loading and request gating.
Axios client uses VITE_API_BASE with cookies and 15s timeout.

storage_systems.usage: Durable moderation logs, timed penalties, reaction rules/logs, private room state, channel lock snapshots, custom commands and audit
Runtime locks, cooldowns, custom command cache, rule cache, queue state, temporary interaction sessions

caching.purpose: Fast lookup on messageCreate without DB roundtrip
Reduce DB/refetch pressure for reaction events
Avoid repeated repository/config reads in private room service
Per-actor per-action throttling and abuse tracking

queues_or_jobs.purpose: Private room stale cleanup and transient message deletion
queues_or_jobs.trigger: Service bootstrap and interaction side effects

queues_or_jobs.purpose: Resynchronize penalty timers with authoritative DB state

data_models.name: mod_logs
relationships: Referenced by log command pagination and moderation audit responses

data_models.name: timed_penalties
relationships: Linked to timed_penalty_role_snapshots by guild_id/user_id for jail restore

data_models.name: text_channel_lock_snapshots
relationships: Used by lock/unlock helpers for restore

env_vars.name: TOKEN
env_vars.purpose: Discord bot token for gateway login

env_vars.name: TARGET_GUILD_ID
env_vars.purpose: Optional hard filter to single guild for events/commands

env_vars.name: DB_HOST/DB_PORT/DB_USER/DB_PASSWORD/DB_NAME
env_vars.purpose: Discrete DB config when DATABASE_URL absent

env_vars.name: DB_SSL
env_vars.purpose: Enable postgres SSL mode in pool config

env_vars.name: PORT
env_vars.purpose: Enable simple health listener returning plain 'ok'

env_vars.name: STATIC_SERVER_CONFIG_JSON
env_vars.purpose: JSON override of static server config

env_vars.name: ABUSE_THRESHOLD/UNAUTH_*
env_vars.purpose: Moderation abuse and unauthorized attempt controls

env_vars.name: VOICE_CONNECT_* and VOICE_RECONNECT_WAIT_TIMEOUT_MS
env_vars.purpose: Voice manager verification and reconnect tuning

env_vars.name: DIAG_MODE
env_vars.purpose: Enable diagnostic structured event logging and leak/timer monitoring

env_vars.name: PERF_MONITOR/PERF_SLOW_QUERY_MS
env_vars.purpose: Enable perf counters and slow query reporting

env_vars.name: VITE_API_BASE/VITE_SINGLE_GUILD_ID/VITE_GUILD_ID
env_vars.purpose: Dashboard client backend target and guild scope behavior

config_files.purpose: Runtime env parsing and clamped numeric/bool defaults
Authoritative static per-guild settings and bindings source-of-truth
Static config normalization, merging, helper accessors
Environment variable template for backend deployment
Environment variable template for frontend build/runtime target

external_integrations.name: Discord API and Gateway
external_integrations.purpose: Receive events, execute moderation actions, manage channels/roles/voice/interactions
external_integrations.rate_limit_handling: Mostly delegated to discord.js internals; command-level local throttles exist for abuse control
external_integrations.retry_strategy: Explicit retries for ban/timeout verification and startup voice joins
external_integrations.timeout_strategy: Voice connect verification timeout; HTTP/REST call timeouts handled by library defaults

external_integrations.name: PostgreSQL
external_integrations.rate_limit_handling: Connection pool max clients; no explicit query rate control
external_integrations.retry_strategy: Startup and selected verification operations include retry for transient errors
external_integrations.timeout_strategy: No explicit per-query timeout observed in DB client config

external_integrations.name: Railway/Supabase/Neon (deployment guidance)
external_integrations.purpose: Operational setup instructions for production environment

secret_handling.method: Secrets loaded from environment variables; .env patterns ignored by git; CI has tracked-file secret scanner.
risks: database.js sets ssl.rejectUnauthorized=false when DB_SSL is enabled, weakening TLS verification against MITM.
secret-scan only checks tracked source/workflow/script files and may miss accidental leakage in untracked or binary artifacts.
dashboard expects cookie-based auth APIs, but scanned backend entrypoint exposes only health endpoint, making auth boundary implementation unclear.

permission_model.mechanism: permissionService.verifyPermission checks command enabled flag, native Discord permissions, optional role gate, safelist, hierarchy, protected targets, and rate limits
risks: Policy drift possible when static config keys/permissions mapping changes without synchronized tests (currently visible in failing permission tests).

permission_model.mechanism: Execution requirement checks for bot capabilities and target manageability/moderatability before mutation
risks: Some flows rely on current member fetch; transient Discord fetch errors can downgrade to generic systemError responses.

permission_model.mechanism: Owner-only interaction checks plus panel-message staleness checks and mutation locks
risks: Large monolithic interaction parser increases review surface for edge-case authorization bugs.

input_validation.area: Prefix command args and target parsing
input_validation.mechanism: Snowflake regex, mention parsing, optional member search, usage templates
gaps: Command parser is manual and command-specific; behavior consistency depends on tests and handler discipline.

input_validation.mechanism: parseRequiredTimeoutDuration with 28d cap and safe buffer
gaps: Duration parser accepts concise tokens only (s/m/h/d) and may reject locale variants.

input_validation.mechanism: Guild/role/channel/emoji existence and duplicate-binding validation at startup
gaps: Validation is startup-time only; runtime drift can still occur after startup.

input_validation.mechanism: Color/url/content length checks and actor binding token
gaps: Pending flow state is in memory only; restart invalidates in-flight UI operations.

abuse_prevention.mechanism: Per-actor command rate limits with abuse counter and optional role removal lock
gaps: Rate-limits are in-memory and process-local; do not scale across multi-instance deployment.

abuse_prevention.mechanism: Unauthorized reply cooldown and temporary block window
gaps: Also process-local and reset on restart.

abuse_prevention.mechanism: Reaction rule cooldown + only-once reservation table
abuse_prevention.coverage: Prevents reaction action storms and duplicate one-time actions
gaps: Cooldown map is in-memory; only-once durability exists but cooldown durability does not.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: PostgreSQL SSL verification disabled when SSL is on
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: database.js config sets ssl.rejectUnauthorized=false for both DATABASE_URL and discrete DB fields.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: TLS MITM or invalid cert acceptance risk in hostile networks.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Use provider CA or system trust chain and set rejectUnauthorized=true by default; allow explicit insecure override only for local dev.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Dashboard/API contract mismatch
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: Dashboard expects many /api endpoints (auth, settings, reaction rules, bot presence, embed send), but scanned backend entrypoint only exposes plain health listener.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Operational UI may be non-functional or may depend on missing/unscanned service; security model of panel endpoints is unclear.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Document and include actual API server implementation in repo, or align dashboard to available backend surface.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Process-local rate limiting and lock state
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: Abuse prevention, cooldowns, and many mutation locks are in-memory only.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Behavior drift and abuse bypass in multi-instance scenarios; restart resets counters and temporary protections.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Move critical throttles/locks to shared storage (Redis/DB advisory locks) if horizontal scale is required.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Broad MessageContent intent usage
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: Bot subscribes to MessageContent intent for prefix/custom command parsing.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Increased data exposure footprint and policy burden for privacy/compliance.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Minimize content handling where possible or migrate to slash interactions for sensitive deployments.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Hardcoded exempt user IDs in tag role sync
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: Specific user IDs bypass tag role sync logic in source code.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Hidden policy exceptions and governance risk; hard to audit operationally.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Move exemptions into auditable config with explicit rationale and expiry tracking.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Health endpoint has no authentication
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: When PORT is set, server returns plain ok for all requests without auth.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Minor exposure of service liveness; can aid reconnaissance.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Bind to internal network or add path token/IP restrictions at ingress.

vulnerabilities_or_risks.title: Large monolithic private room service
vulnerabilities_or_risks.description: Single ~3k-line module handles many access and mutation paths.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.impact: Higher chance of hidden authorization/logic edge cases and harder security review.
vulnerabilities_or_risks.recommendation: Split into domain modules (access policy, panel routing, overwrite sync, lifecycle orchestration) with isolated tests.

error_handling.global_strategy: Process-level fail-fast on uncaughtException/unhandledRejection with structured logging and graceful resource shutdown.
local_patterns: executeModerationAction centralizes primary error mapping and side-effect degradation.
Many Discord event handlers wrap internals in try/catch and log contextual errors.
Startup gates use withRetry/waitForStartupGate for transient failures.
State verification retries used after moderation mutations (ban/timeout).
gaps: No dead-letter queue for repeatedly failing scheduled operations.
Many transient runtime errors are only logged; no alerting escalation in-code.
Some user-facing failures collapse to generic systemError templates.

logging_observability.logging: Custom logger supports text/json formats; contextual structured errors; diagnostics channel under DIAG_MODE.
logging_observability.metrics: Optional lightweight perf monitor counters/log lines; no persistent metrics backend detected.
logging_observability.alerting: No native alerting integration detected in repository.

recovery_patterns.details: DB migrations, lock acquisition, static config validation, and service bootstraps retry transient failures.
Ban/unban and timeout actions verify final remote state with retries before emitting success.
Penalty scheduler regularly re-loads active rows and re-schedules timers.
Per-target/per-channel/per-room locks prevent racey concurrent state changes.

performance_and_scalability.current_scaling_model: Single runtime process with PostgreSQL advisory lock singleton. Horizontal scaling is intentionally constrained at bot gateway level.

bottlenecks.title: Private room service monolith on hot voice path
bottlenecks.description: voiceStateUpdate and interaction handling funnel through a large module with substantial branching and DB/Discord API calls.
bottlenecks.impact: CPU/event-loop pressure and maintenance complexity in high voice churn guilds.

bottlenecks.title: No sharding strategy for large guild/event scale
bottlenecks.description: Single client instance without shard orchestration.
bottlenecks.impact: Gateway and cache limits can become bottlenecks as guild/member/event volume grows.

bottlenecks.title: In-memory-only throttles/caches
bottlenecks.description: Rate limits/cooldowns/resettable runtime state live only in memory.
bottlenecks.impact: Restart-induced burst windows and poor multi-instance consistency.

bottlenecks.title: Frequent authoritative fetches
bottlenecks.description: Many commands re-fetch members/bans/channels for correctness.
bottlenecks.impact: Higher Discord REST call volume and latency under command spikes.

hot_paths.notes: Critical path for all prefix command interactions.
Potentially highest-frequency event path in active voice communities.
Rule cache quality strongly impacts latency and DB pressure.

performance_and_scalability.rate_limit_strategy: Per-user per-action local counters in cache.js plus warning cooldown and abuse threshold actions; reaction engine has per-rule per-user cooldown.
performance_and_scalability.concurrency_model: Node async event loop with explicit per-key locks (targets, rooms, channels, bans) and per-guild promise queue for voice operations.
performance_and_scalability.notes: The design favors correctness and deterministic moderation outcomes over raw throughput.

strengths: Strong test coverage breadth across moderation, reaction, private room, startup, and utility modules.
Centralized action execution and permission services reduce repeated error-handling boilerplate.
Static config normalization and validation pipeline is explicit and robust.
Post-action authoritative verification reduces false success reports.
Rich operational diagnostics and retry utilities are present for unstable dependencies.
weaknesses: privateRoomService is very large and handles multiple concerns in one file.
Moderation command handlers still duplicate substantial preflight/postflight patterns.
Mojibake/encoding artifacts in user-facing strings reduce clarity and increase test brittleness.
Dashboard and backend runtime contract appears inconsistent in scanned code.
Some dead/legacy-like structures remain (for example builtin command names and repository modules without active call paths).

duplication_areas.description: Repeated moderation command scaffolding around verifyPermission, consumeLimit, executeModerationAction options, and DM notifications
Interaction custom-id parsing and guard branches concentrated in one large private room module
Template text defaults and command-specific variants are duplicated in static catalog structures

technical_debt.title: Monolithic private room domain service
technical_debt.description: A single file handles persistence cache, access policy, interaction routing, overwrite sync, lifecycle cleanup and logging.
technical_debt.recommendation: Split by bounded context (room repository adapter, policy engine, panel router, overwrite engine, cleanup scheduler) and add module-level contracts.

technical_debt.title: Dashboard/backend API surface drift
technical_debt.description: Frontend expects auth/settings/reaction/embed APIs not visible in scanned backend runtime entrypoint.
technical_debt.recommendation: Consolidate HTTP API implementation in repository or adjust dashboard contracts and docs.

technical_debt.title: Permission policy/test mismatch currently failing
technical_debt.description: Permission tests indicate drift in command gate vs hierarchy behavior expectations.
technical_debt.recommendation: Finalize policy intent (native permission-first vs role gate) and update either implementation or tests consistently.

technical_debt.title: Unused or partially wired modules
technical_debt.description: commandRepository exists without observed runtime call sites; builtin command names include vcmute/vcunmute without active command handlers.
technical_debt.recommendation: Remove dead paths or fully wire/document intended flows.

technical_debt.title: Encoding and localization consistency issues
technical_debt.description: Mixed mojibake and diacritic variants in user-facing strings produce brittle assertions and inconsistent UX.
technical_debt.recommendation: Normalize UTF-8 string sources, enforce lint/test snapshot rules for localization text.

testing.coverage_estimate: Medium-high behavioral coverage for backend core modules and key regressions; no formal coverage percentage report detected.
critical_untested_areas: Full production auth/session HTTP API paths for dashboard (not found in scanned backend runtime surface).
True multi-process/horizontal-scale behavior for rate limits and locks.
End-to-end Discord integration under high event volume and real network faults.
Formal migration rollback/forward compatibility tests beyond startup schema checks.

hosting: Generic Node host with external supervisor (pm2/systemd/docker restart policy)
Railway-oriented deployment guidance exists (in docs, not as runtime config files)

containerization.details: No Dockerfile or compose file detected in repository.

ci_cd.details: GitHub Actions CI runs secret scan, lint, tests/build, and format checks for api and dashboard on Node 20.

release_process.detected: CI-gated branch/pull-request validation; no automated deployment workflow detected in repo.
release_process.notes: Operations docs instruct using external process supervisors and optional Railway DB setup.

runtime_requirements: Discord bot token and target guild static config
PostgreSQL database reachable with required runtime tables
Outbound network access to Discord gateway/REST and PostgreSQL
For dashboard usage, an HTTP API server implementing /api/* endpoints expected by frontend
ops_risks: Single-process singleton model limits scaling and creates single point of failure.
If advisory lock acquisition fails repeatedly, startup blocks until lock becomes available.
Current API/dashboard contract uncertainty can break operations UI in production.
No built-in alerting/metrics backend; incident detection depends on log monitoring.

startup_sequence: Load env config and initialize logging/diagnostics.
Run DB ping + idempotent schema migration.
Create Discord client and instantiate feature services.
Optionally start health server on PORT.
Acquire PostgreSQL advisory bot instance lock.
Validate static guild config against live resources.
Bootstrap penalty scheduler/private rooms/reaction cache/tag sync.
Set startup phase completed and continue event loop.
command_execution_sequence: Prefix check and command parse in moderation.handlePrefix.
verifyPermission applies gates, hierarchy, and rate check.
check.consumeLimit acquires/consumes execution receipt when needed.
Side effects (DB logs, scheduler actions, DM notify) run with degraded warning behavior if partial failures.
database_interaction_sequence: database.execute converts ? placeholders to PostgreSQL $n safely.
Repositories/services run SQL via pooled client.
Mutating commands frequently verify authoritative state by re-fetching Discord resources and DB records.
Scheduler and cache bootstrap reload active records at startup.
error_recovery_sequence: Local handler catches error and logs context.
Known command errors map to specific template responses.
Transient operations retry through utility retry wrappers.
Fatal uncaught errors trigger graceful shutdown and process exit for external restart supervisor.

reverse_engineered_logic.core_loop_explanation: The bot runs an event-driven loop around discord.js Client listeners. Every incoming gateway event is filtered by guild scope, routed to a domain handler, and optionally persisted to PostgreSQL. Moderation commands share a strict pipeline: parse -> permission/hierarchy/rate gate -> execution wrapper -> side effects -> templated response -> optional target DM. Long-lived runtime state is split between durable DB rows (penalties, rules, rooms, logs) and process-local maps (locks, cooldowns, caches). Startup performs schema reconciliation and service bootstrapping; shutdown tears down timers, client, DB, and advisory lock.
how_a_new_command_is_added: Create handler module in api/src/bot/commands/<name>.js exporting run(ctx).
Register handler in commandHandlers map in api/src/bot/moderation.js.
Add command metadata/template defaults in api/src/application/messages/catalog.js if custom messaging needed.
Add action bucket mapping in actionNormalization.js and permission static settings keys if rate/role gates differ.
Add tests in api/test/moderation.<name>.command.test.js plus permission/partial-failure cases.
how_an_event_is_processed: Event listener in api/src/discordClient.js receives payload.
Early guards skip bots, non-guild contexts, or non-target guild IDs.
Service lookup via injected getter retrieves current service instance.
Handler executes with try/catch and logs contextual failures without crashing process.
how_state_is_read_written: Read static immutable config from config/static layer (with optional env JSON override).
Read/write runtime state through SQL repositories and scheduler/service modules.
Keep hot caches/locks in memory to reduce DB pressure and serialize mutations.
Persist key snapshots (channel lock state, private room lock/visibility, jail role snapshots) for recovery after restart.
how_failures_propagate: Command-level expected errors become specific user templates; unexpected errors become systemError templates and logs.
Side-effect failures usually downgrade success with warning message rather than hard fail after primary action.
Startup dependency failures retry with backoff; unrecoverable startup failure exits process.
Unhandled process errors trigger controlled shutdown and non-zero exit for supervisor restart.
how_permissions_are_checked: Command enabled flag from authoritative static settings.
Native Discord actor permission checks (ModerateMembers/KickMembers/BanMembers etc.) or configured role gate.
Optional safelist bypasses for specific flows (for lock policy cases).
Actor/target/bot hierarchy and protected target checks through nativeHierarchy and role safety.
Rate limit check and consume with rollback-aware receipt mechanism.

comparison_ready_snapshot.architectural_maturity_tier: Tier 3/5 (Production-capable single-instance bot with strong safeguards, but limited scale architecture and some contract/debt gaps)
primary_strengths: Robust moderation correctness model (authoritative verify + mutation locks + rollback-aware execution).
Comprehensive runtime table schema and startup migration hardening.
Good automated regression test breadth over core behavior.
Strong static config validation against live Discord resources.
Operational resilience patterns (startup retries, graceful shutdown, diagnostics).
primary_weaknesses: Very large private room service module with high complexity.
Dashboard/API contract uncertainty in scanned backend surface.
No sharding/horizontal scalability model beyond singleton lock.
In-memory-only rate limits/cooldowns/locks for many behaviors.
Current test suite has 19 failing backend tests (as executed on 2026-04-10).
most_important_gaps_vs_high_quality_bots: Distributed state/lock/rate-limit layer for safe multi-instance operation.
Clear, documented, versioned control-plane HTTP API with auth and RBAC.
Smaller bounded-context modules replacing giant orchestration files.
Formalized deployment manifests and reproducible release automation.
comparison_ready_snapshot.one_paragraph_assessment: This codebase has many production-minded choices for correctness (state verification, retries, mutation locks, schema migration safety) and unusually strong behavior tests for a Discord bot project. Its main structural constraints are scalability and maintainability: it intentionally runs as a singleton, keeps critical throttles in memory, and concentrates private-room complexity in a monolith. The dashboard appears to target a broader API surface than what is visible in the scanned backend runtime entrypoint, which is the biggest operational clarity risk.

unknowns_and_assumptions.topic: Backend HTTP API implementation for dashboard endpoints
unknowns_and_assumptions.notes: Dashboard requests many /api/* routes, but scanned api/src runtime entrypoint only exposes a plain health listener. It is unknown whether API server code exists outside scanned paths or in another service/repo.
Railway and external supervisor guidance exists in docs, but no deployment manifest confirms actual platform.

unknowns_and_assumptions.topic: Real-world guild scale and event volume
unknowns_and_assumptions.notes: No production traffic metrics or shard strategy metadata present in repo.

unknowns_and_assumptions.topic: Whether vcmute/vcunmute commands are intentionally removed or temporarily disconnected
unknowns_and_assumptions.notes: Names appear in builtin command list/templates/action maps, but no active command handlers were found in moderation dispatcher.

unknowns_and_assumptions.topic: Current intent of failing backend tests
unknowns_and_assumptions.notes: 19 failing tests suggest ongoing policy refactor/drift; unclear whether tests or code are source of truth.

unknowns_and_assumptions.topic: Security posture of runtime secrets outside tracked files
unknowns_and_assumptions.notes: Git ignores .env files; repository scanner checks tracked files. Runtime secret handling depends on deployment process not fully represented in repo.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Defines startup, singleton locking, service bootstrapping, and shutdown semantics.
important_findings: Uses PostgreSQL advisory lock BOT_INSTANCE_LOCK_NAME for singleton behavior.
Runs migrations and static config validation before fully ready.
Only built-in HTTP listener is plain health endpoint on PORT.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Central event wiring and flow dispatch.
important_findings: Prefix commands are prioritized before custom commands.
Interaction routing splits embed builder and private room service.
TARGET_GUILD_ID scope filters most events.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Primary command parser and dispatcher.
important_findings: Static command map drives prefix command execution.
Template sender used for standardized command responses.
Target resolution has unresolved-ID mode for ban command path.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Authorization, hierarchy, abuse/rate controls.
important_findings: Combines enabled gates, native permissions, optional role checks, hierarchy checks, and bot capability checks.
Implements unauthorized reply throttling and abuse counter.
Produces rollback-capable limit consumption receipts.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Core command execution and failure semantics.
important_findings: Centralized primary action error mapping and side-effect degradation behavior.
Per-target mutation lock with timeout.
Commit/rollback handling for consumed rate-limit receipts.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Most complex moderation flow, showing correctness strategy.
important_findings: Uses authoritative timeout verification with retries.
If voice disconnect fails after timeout, attempts rollback and reports specific failure template.
Integrates required bot MoveMembers preflight for voice targets.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Ban correctness and mutation locking behavior.
important_findings: Uses guildBanState authoritative checks and mutation lock.
Success depends on verified ban presence, not just API call completion.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Unban correctness and stale cache invalidation.
important_findings: Uses authoritative absence verification and cache eviction.
Handles unknown-ban Discord errors as notApplied path.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Time-based deferred action model.
important_findings: DB-backed schedule with interval reconciliation.
Uses row re-read before revoke to avoid stale timer actions.
Supports jail role snapshot restore via timed_penalty_role_snapshots.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Reaction automation flow and constraints.
important_findings: In-memory rule cache + miss cache + cooldown map.
Only-once guard persisted in DB table.
Supports role mutations, DM/reply, channel links, internal command, grouped reaction cleanup.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Largest and most complex subsystem.
important_findings: Manages room lifecycle, panel UI, lock/visibility snapshots, permit/reject lists, and cleanup.
Uses multiple lock sets for room/owner/whitelist mutation serialization.
Persists room state and logs to PostgreSQL.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Voice connection reliability and queueing model.
important_findings: Per-guild operation queue serializes connection operations.
Connect success requires state or authoritative member-voice verification.
Reconnect handling attempts recovery before destroying connection.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Identity-driven role automation and policy exceptions.
important_findings: Uses primaryGuild identity flags to decide role membership.
Contains hardcoded exempt user IDs.
Per-member lock and rerun queue reduce race conditions.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: DB connectivity and SQL abstraction details.
important_findings: Question-mark placeholders converted to PostgreSQL $n placeholders with quote/comment awareness.
SSL mode sets rejectUnauthorized=false when enabled.
Exposes pool-level query and connection abstractions.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Runtime schema ownership and migration semantics.
important_findings: Creates all runtime tables idempotently at startup.
Archives legacy config tables and drops retired runtime tables.
Adds unique constraints/indexes for active penalty and audit lookup behavior.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Defines static policy and role/channel IDs.
important_findings: Static per-guild settings include moderation gates, role bindings, and private VC settings.
Defaults and bot presence are immutable object literals.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Config normalization and runtime access model.
important_findings: Supports STATIC_SERVER_CONFIG_JSON override.
buildAuthoritativeSettings overlays runtime dynamic settings under static keys, preserving static authority for controlled keys.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Prevents startup with invalid guild role/channel/emoji bindings.
important_findings: Checks duplicate bindings and missing resources.
Validates enabled feature required settings and channel type constraints.
Treats some startup voice issues as warnings not hard errors.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Defines operational UI API contract and data flow assumptions.
important_findings: Expects auth/session, guild resources, settings, reaction-rules, bot-presence, embed send, health endpoints.
Client-side validations for reaction rule payload before API submit.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: CI/CD and quality gates.
important_findings: Runs secret scan, lint, tests/build, format checks.
Node version pinned to 20 for CI jobs.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Current reliability and policy drift signal.
important_findings: Many failing assertions indicate ongoing divergence between expected and actual permission gate/hierarchy behavior.

evidence_index.reason_relevant: Text encoding consistency signal.
important_findings: Current failing test indicates default catalog text expectation drift/encoding normalization changes.

final_assessment.what_this_bot_is: A production-oriented Discord moderation and automation backend with static policy control, PostgreSQL durability, strict action verification, and extensive domain-specific test coverage.
final_assessment.what_it_is_not: It is not a horizontally-scaled/sharded distributed bot platform, and in the scanned state it is not a clearly complete full-stack control-plane API implementation for the bundled dashboard.
top_5_strengths: Authoritative post-action verification for critical moderation actions (mute/ban/unban).
Strong startup hardening: config validation, schema migrations, retry/backoff, singleton lock.
Deep feature set beyond moderation: reaction rules, private rooms, tag role sync, voice startup connect.
Comprehensive behavior tests across many edge cases and race conditions.
Well-defined static config normalization and validation pipeline.
top_5_issues: Dashboard to backend API contract appears incomplete or split across missing code.
privateRoomService complexity is very high and concentrated in one large file.
No sharding/distributed state model for high scale.
Critical throttles/locks are process-local in memory.
Backend test suite currently has 19 failures, many around permission/ban/encoding drift.
top_5_refactor_priorities: Decompose privateRoomService into bounded modules with narrower responsibilities.
Standardize moderation command scaffold into reusable declarative command specs.
Resolve permissionService policy drift and stabilize with contract tests.
Consolidate/verify dashboard HTTP API implementation and auth model in repository.
Normalize localization/encoding strings and enforce UTF-8 consistency checks.
top_5_scaling_priorities: Introduce shard/distributed gateway strategy if guild/event scale requires it.
Move rate-limit/cooldown state to shared store for multi-instance safety.
Profile and optimize voiceStateUpdate/private-room hot path.
Add query timeouts and structured DB performance telemetry.
Reduce repetitive authoritative fetches with short-lived validated caches where safe.
top_5_security_priorities: Fix PostgreSQL TLS verification to reject invalid certs by default.
Document and enforce a complete authenticated API boundary for dashboard operations.
Move hardcoded policy exceptions (tag role exemptions) into auditable config.
Harden health endpoint exposure through network controls.
Expand secret scanning and runtime secret hygiene policy beyond tracked source files.
final_assessment.executive_summary: The bot backend is technically mature for single-instance operation and correctness-sensitive moderation, with robust startup resilience and strong regression tests. Its main risks are architectural: concentrated complexity in private room logic, process-local state that limits safe horizontal scaling, and ambiguity around the dashboard's expected HTTP API surface. Addressing these areas would significantly improve maintainability, security confidence, and growth capacity.
